companyDescription:
Europe’s largest private forest owner manufacturing wood products, pulp, packaging paper and renewable energy. 

The core of SCA’s business is the growing forest, Europe’s largest private forest holding. Around this unique resource, we have built a well-developed value chain based on renewable raw material from our own and others’ forests. We offer packaging paper, pulp, wood products, renewable energy, services for forest owners and efficient transport solutions. 

2024 the forest products company SCA had approximately 3,400 employees and sales amounted to approximately SEK 20 bn. SCA was founded in 1929 and has its headquarters in Sundsvall, Sweden. 

Offcial LinkedIn account for SCA.
descriptionHtml:
Placering

Sundsvall, SE

Ett hållbart arbetsliv

På SCA har du möjlighet att göra skillnad på riktigt. Med skogens kraft bidrar vi till en hållbar framtid och hjälper våra kunder över hela världen att minska sina fossila avtryck. Vill du utvecklas och tänka nytt så kommer du att trivas hos oss, för vi ger alla möjlighet att nå sin fulla potential. Vi vet att det är människorna som gör framgångarna!

Inom Business Services Finans arbetar vi mot målet att vara SCAs kompetenscenter för redovisning och rapportering samt med att leverera operationella ekonomi- och masterdatatjänster av högsta kvalitet i globala och värdeskapande processer, till hela bolaget. Som börsnoterat bolag är kraven på ekonomifunktionen höga och vi är mitt inne i en spännande transformationsresa där det pågår utveckling av människor, processer och system.

Nu söker vi vår nästa Accountant till Accounts Payable.

Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

Din nästa utmaning

Inom Business Services Finans arbetar ett av våra team, Accounts Payable, i inköpsprocessen med hantering av leverantörsfakturor. Vårt mål är att leverera högklassig service till SCA och våra leverantörer – och du blir i rollen som Accountant en viktig spelare i vårt team.

Som Accountant inom Accounts Payable ansvarar du för att säkerställa att våra leverantörsfakturor hanteras korrekt, i tid och enligt gällande regelverk. I arbetet ingår bland annat löpande hantering av leverantörsfakturor, avvikelsehantering, påminnelsehantering och bokslutsarbete.

Du förväntas vara delaktig i olika typer av utredningsarbete samt bidra till att vidareutveckla och ständigt förbättra rutiner och arbetssätt inom leverantörsfakturaområdet. Du bidrar även till att säkerställa god intern styrning och kontroll samtidigt som du ger våra intressenter god service.

Vem är du?

Vi söker dig som har utbildning på minst gymnasial nivå med några års arbetslivserfarenhet eller relevant eftergymnasial utbildning. Tidigare erfarenhet av arbete inom redovisning, löpande bokföring och/eller hantering av reskontra är meriterande. Vi ser också att du har god systemvana och ett genuint tekniskt intresse. Vidare behärskar du svenska och engelska mycket väl i tal och skrift.

Som person är du initiativtagande, lösningsorienterad och kan snabbt sätta dig in i nya frågeställningar. Du är även noggrann och har god samarbets- och kommunikationsförmåga. Du trivs i en miljö med hög förändringstakt, har positiv inställning och vill utvecklas tillsammans med oss.

På SCA arbetar vi efter värdeorden Ansvar, Högklassighet och Respekt. Vi söker dig vars beteende samspelar med dessa värdeord och som vill bidra till en sund kultur på arbetsplatsen.

Vad erbjuder vi dig?

Vi arbetar aktivitetsbaserat i helt nya moderna lokaler anpassade till vår verksamhet och framtiden. För oss är en hälsosam och säker arbetsmiljö en självklarhet. Hos oss får du stora möjligheter att växa och utvecklas. Vi uppmuntrar till kompetensutveckling, till att ta ansvar och till att du förverkligar dina idéer. På SCA är vi måna om varandra och vi erbjuder ett öppet, stöttande arbetsklimat. Vi förstår också vikten av ett hållbart arbetsliv.

Som anställd hos oss får du jobba med den allra senaste tekniken och vara en del i omställningen till ett hållbart samhälle. Våra skogar och de produkter vi sätter på marknaden bidrar med en klimatnytta större än utsläppen från Sveriges alla personbilar under ett år. Vi tar vara på precis varje del av de träd som vi avverkar och producerar produkter från skogen som bidrar till att motverka klimatförändringarna. Att göra skillnad på riktigt ger arbetet en extra dimension.

Vill du veta mer?

Har du frågor om rollen så är du välkommen att kontakta Jennie Kjellin, gruppchef Accounts Payables, jennie.kjellin@sca.com

Frågor kring rekryteringsprocessen besvaras av Nina Sterning-Näslund, HR specialist rekrytering, nina.sterning@sca.com

Fackliga Företrädare

Maria Jonsson, Unionen, maria.jonsson@sca.com

Martin Holmvall, Akademikerföreningen, martin.holmvall@sca.com

Vi jobbar med löpande urval och tillsättning så skicka din ansökan omgående så kanske vi ses snart!

Sista ansökan är 22/9-2025

I samband med intervju kontrollerar vi giltig identitetshandling.

Om SCA

Kärnan i SCAs verksamhet är skogen, 2,7 miljoner hektar i norra Sverige, Estland, Lettland och Litauen. Kring denna unika resurs har vi byggt en välutvecklad värdekedja baserad på förnybar råvara från våra egna och andras skogar.

SCA har en effektiv och integrerad värdekedja med ett miljöcertifierat skogsbruk och moderna produktionsanläggningar belägna i anslutning till skogsinnehavet. SCAs snabbt växande skog binder koldioxid, bolagets produkter bidrar till ett mer hållbart samhälle och den förnybara fibern kan ersätta produkter baserade på fossila råvaror.

För mer information, besök sca.com SCA - Vi drivs av skogens kraft

Läs mer om SCA som arbetsplats på SCA som arbetsgivare – SCA

Följ våra ambassadörer på SCA (@wearescasweden)

  • Foton och videor på Instagram

Lyssna på några av våra medarbetare på Spotify: SCA

companyDescription:
CareDx: Transforming Transplant Patient Care Through Novel Surveillance Management Solutions

CareDx, Inc. is dedicated to improving the lives of organ transplant patients through non-invasive diagnostics. By combining the latest advances in genomics and bioinformatics technology, with a commitment to generating high quality clinical evidence through trials and registries, CareDx is at the forefront of organ transplant surveillance and pre-transplant HLA typing solutions.

NASDAQ:CDNA

About AlloSure®

AlloSure is the first and only non-invasive blood test that directly measures allograft injury and identifies the probability of active rejection to better manage kidney transplant patients. AlloSure is a clinical-grade, proprietary Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) based test to detect donor-derived cell-free DNA (dd-cfDNA) in order to identify organ injury in kidney transplant recipients. AlloSure is analytically validated as a sensitive, specific, and precise measurement of dd-cfDNA. AlloSure detects active rejection (acute active ABMR, chronic active ABMR, or TCMR) with high accuracy, outperforms serum creatinine in kidney transplant recipients, and is highly sensitive in distinguishing ABMR from no ABMR. AlloSure is performed in the CareDx CLIA-certified laboratory. 

http://www.allosure.com/


About AlloMap® 

AlloMap Molecular Expression Testing is a non-invasive blood test used to aid in the identification of heart transplant recipients with stable allograft function who have a low probability of moderate/severe acute cellular rejection at the time of testing in conjunction with standard clinical assessment. AlloMap testing measures the expression levels of 20 genes from a blood sample. The combined expression of these genes is represented as an AlloMap test score. AlloMap is performed in the CareDx CLIA-certified laboratory and has been commercially available in the United States since 2005. AlloMap was cleared by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in 2008 and was CE marked for the European Union in 2011. Use of AlloMap is also included in the International Society for Heart and Lung Transplantation (ISHLT) Practice Guidelines, published in August 2010, the worldwide standard for the care of heart transplant patients.

http://www.allomap.com/
companySlogan: Creating life-changing solutions that enable transplant patients to thrive.
descriptionHtml:
CareDx, Inc. is a leading precision medicine solutions company focused on the discovery, development, and commercialization of clinically differentiated, high-value healthcare solutions for transplant patients and caregivers. CareDx offers products, testing services, and digital healthcare solutions along the pre- and post-transplant patient journey, and is the leading provider of genomics-based information for transplant patients.

We are seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Finance Accountant to join our team in Stockholm. This role requires background in finance or accounting and hands-on experience with NetSuite or similar ERP systems. The successful candidate will be well-organized, proactive, and capable of working in a fast-paced international environment.

This role is based on-site in Stockholm, with a requirement to work from the office at least three days per week

Responsibilities:

  • Prepare salary documentation for employees across multiple countries and coordinate with local payroll providers
  • Review payroll documentation and process salary payments
  • Review and process employee expense reports
  • Book vendor invoices and manage approval workflows via Centsoft
  • Match purchase orders to invoices
  • Prepare payment proposals for accounts payable
  • Amortize vendor invoices
  • Reconcile accounts and support month-end close
  • Book received customer payments and manage collections
  • Perform intercompany reconciliations

Italicized tasks are performed as backup during vacation periods.

Requirements:

  • Fluent in English and Swedish (required); German or Spanish is a plus
  • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office
  • Experience with NetSuite or other integrated ERP systems
  • Prior experience in an international company
  • Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills
  • Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities

descriptionHtml:
We're Hiring: Accounting Lead

Hem is looking for a hands-on and proactive Accounting Lead to take the helm of our day-to-day financial operations. This is a key role in our finance team — you'll oversee everything from monthly closings and VAT filings to payroll coordination and cash flow planning.

If you're someone who thrives in fast-paced, detail-oriented environments, loves working cross-functionally, and wants to be part of a small, ambitious team building something meaningful — then keep reading.

What You’ll Do

As the Accounting Lead, you’ll be the go-to person for all things accounting. On a monthly basis, you’ll lead the closing process for our two companies (a Swedish AB and a US Inc), making sure every krona and dollar is accounted for. You’ll prepare our financial statements and support the development of our monthly management reports in collaboration with our CFO, providing insight into how the business is performing. Basically, you’ll be the backbone of our finance operations, with ownership across all core accounting processes:

  • Daily & Weekly Ops: Oversee day-to-day bookkeeping and inbox management, coordinate with our Junior Finance Coordinator, reconcile the bank (AB & Inc), manage AP/AR, and drive our weekly payments cycle. You’ll ensure suppliers are paid on schedule and customers pay us. If a big client is late, you’re on it – sending a friendly nudge and keeping an eye on our cash flow.
  • Monthly Close: Coordinate the entire closing schedule for both our Swedish and US entities – including reconciliations, posting accruals, TP invoicing, commissions, and royalty calculations, as well as double-checking that our revenues and expenses are spot on.
  • Liquidity Planning: Prepare our weekly liquidity analysis, including a forward-looking assessment of incoming/outgoing cash. You’ll play a key role in helping the company plan for upcoming payments and maintain a healthy cash position.
  • Tax & Compliance: Take charge of our tax calendar – that means VAT returns in Sweden, Poland, the UK, and Portugal, as well as keeping us compliant with sales tax in various US states. Deadlines don’t scare you; you meet them with time to spare.
  • Payroll & Benefits: Coordinate payroll with external providers, expense reimbursements, and pension reporting. You’ll also maintain our inventory list of company assets like laptops and phones.
  • Annual Closing & Audit: Own the operational side of our annual close and audit process. Act as point of contact with auditors, gather documentation, and support the CFO in preparing the Annual Report.
  • Cross-Team Collaboration: Work closely with the CFO on forecasting and budgeting – giving input on what the latest numbers mean for our future. Partner with other teams (Sales, Operations, Creative) to make sure finance is supporting their needs – whether it’s setting up a new customer in the system, or providing cost info for a project.

Who You Are

  • You have 2+ years of experience in accounting or financial operations — ideally with exposure to international reporting (Sweden + US is a plus!).
  • You’re comfortable owning accounting processes end to end and love organizing structure and systems – closing the books is second nature to you, and you have a knack for spotting when “something doesn’t look right” in the numbers.
  • You have excellent attention to detail and aren’t afraid of a spreadsheet or ERP (we use Microsoft Navision).
  • You’re used to handling multiple timelines and deliverables across jurisdictions, and you take deadlines seriously – when you say “it’ll be done by Monday,” we know it will (and done well).
  • You communicate clearly and casually – in other words, you can distill financial lingo into plain English for the team.
  • You’re curious, humble, and collaborative. You like being part of a tight team and don’t mind rolling up your sleeves.

Why Join Us:

At Hem, we blend creativity with solid business sense. As the Accounting Lead, you’ll sit at the crossroads of our innovative design projects and the numbers that fuel them. You’ll work directly with a CFO who values your expertise and treats you as a partner. We offer a friendly, inclusive atmosphere where your voice is heard – whether you’re proposing a new software tool or a team lunch spot.

If you’re looking for a role where you can take ownership, grow your skills, and see the impact of your work every month, come join our journey. We promise a dynamic workplace, opportunities to drive improvement, and a team of passionate individuals who are as committed to design as they are to good finance practices.

Ready to lead our numbers game? Apply now and let’s build something great together.

companyDescription:
We gather the best from two worlds.

With a clear objective of technology first - yet banking at heart - our goal is to be the leader in embedded finance in Europe. A strong entrepreneurial spirit combined with extensive knowledge financial technology development gives us a good understanding of how to navigate and perform in the fast-growing fintech sector. 
We are bridging the gap between the new and the old bank era by offering technologically and financing solutions where demand is increasing.

Bankaktiebolaget Nordiska (publ) is a Swedish credit institution operating under the supervision of Sweden’s Financial Supervisory Authority (Finansinspektionen). From our offices in Stockholm and Sundsvall we are currently servicing several BaaS customers in Sweden, Norway, Finland, Denmark and the Netherlands. Furthermore we offer deposit accounts in Sweden and Germany that are covered by the Swedish government Deposit Guarantee Scheme.  

We are constantly seeking new talents who can be part of our journey to deliver tomorrow's banking services today.
companySlogan: We are challenging the new bank era with profitable financing and powerful technology
descriptionHtml:
About Nordiska

Nordiska is a bank that provides innovative financial products for both companies and consumers. Nordiska Embedded is a platform for built-in financial services, where we offer savings, loans or payment services, either under our own brand or through partners. Nordiska also provide corporate and real estate financing as well as sustainable savings with a government deposit guarantee.

What We Offer

Our code of conduct is the foundation for everything we do. We act with honesty and responsibility to build long-term trust among customers, partners, and employees. We believe that diversity drives innovation and encourage each employee to contribute their unique expertise and perspective. We are driven and dedicated to making a difference. Our employees receive a competitive benefits package.

Purpose of the role

The Head of Group Accounting is a key leadership role responsible for overseeing the Groups accounting and financial reporting. This role ensures accurate financial reporting, regulatory compliance, and strategic financial planning. The role leads the Group Accounting team based in Stockholm with operations primarily in the Nordic countries. This role plays a crucial part in maintaining financial integrity and supporting overall Group financial operations. The role reports to the Group CFO.

Responsibilities

  • Lead the preparation and be responsible for the monthly, quarterly, and annual closings and financial statements in accordance with IFRS/local GAAP for the Group.
  • Responsible for the Groups quarterly and annual report.
  • Manage the external audit process and liaise with auditors and regulators.
  • Ensure timely and accurate consolidation of Group financials.
  • Responsible for and overseeing the day to day accounting operations, leading and coordinating the team(s) within the Groups organization, including tasks such as vacation planning, approving team’s expenses, absences and vacation requests.
  • Responsible for identifying competency needs and planning competency development for the team.
  • Reponsible for and ensuring all financial transactions and documents align with laws and regulations.
  • Streamline accounting functions and processes to ensure financial accuracy and compliance, while implementing continuous improvements in daily operations.
  • Responsible for ensuring correct handling of VAT and taxes for the specific company.
  • Provide ad hoc financial analysis and forecasting.
  • Maintain and update the Groups accounting manual.
  • Group responsibility for the Groups ERP system (NetSuite).

Application and process

We will conduct ongoing interviews during the process. For the final candidate, we also perform a background check. For questions about the role and more information, you are warmly welcome to contact Henrik Wennerholm, henrik.wennerholm@nordiska.com.

companyDescription:
John Bean Technologies Corporation (JBT) is a leading global technology solutions provider to high-value segments of the food and beverage industry with a focus on proteins, liquid foods, and automated guided vehicles (AGVs).

Food and Beverage Solutions – Our equipment offerings include primary, secondary and further value-added processing, including chilling, mixing/grinding, injecting, blending, marinating, tumbling, flattening, forming, portioning, coating, cooking, frying, freezing, extracting, pasteurizing, sterilizing, concentrating, high pressure processing, weighing, inspecting, filling, closing, sealing, and packaging, which support a large and growing portfolio of food, beverage, and health end markets.

Automated Guided Vehicle Systems – Our Automated Guided Vehicle Systems offerings include stand-alone, fully integrated, and dual-mode robotic systems for material movement requirements with a wide variety of applications including automotive manufacturing, warehousing, and medical facilities.
companySlogan: A global leader that designs, develops and delivers solutions for high-value segments of the food & beverage industries.
descriptionHtml:
Overview ...

At JBT Marel, what we do matters. As one of the global leaders within food and beverage technology and automated systems, we know that the contribution of our employees, ultimately leads to the success of our business fortifying the future of food.

We are committed to our employees and will provide you with development and opportunities to allow you to be the best version of yourself at work, at home, and in your community.

We foster a genuine inclusive team culture enjoying collaborative working across our global teams to deliver world-class projects.

We encourage development ensuring new experiences and challenges at JBT Marel in order to feed your growth!

We’re looking for an Accountant to our Finance department in Helsingborg, Sweden!

The Opportunity ...

Are you an Accountant with a solid knowledge of K3, ready for a next and exciting step in your career? Look no further, we have a fantastic opportunity waiting for you in JBT Marel!

Responsibilities

We are looking for an Accountant to join our team in Helsingborg. The main areas of responsibilities will be:

  • Timely and accurate reporting of JBT AB on a monthly basis, both from Swedish and US Gaap perspective including preparation and review of journal entries and account reconciliations
  • Actively participate in monthly balance sheet reviews working with the business and SSC (Shared Service Centre)
  • VAT reporting and support to the orginization with VAT, TAX and other legal enquiries
  • Support internal and external audit requests and participate in efforts to remediate audit and control findings
  • Document standard operating procedures and instructions surrounding accounting procedures
  • Corporate governance for JBT AB including support with Annual report, work with external service providers on the Income Tax declaration and assist on board changes
  • Intercompany compliance such as monthly reconciliations and transfer pricing agreements
  • Share best practices in accounting practices with others in the Accounting CoE and support the Accounting Manager to implement them within the team


Besides this, included in the areas of responsibilities lays communication with Tax Authorities and Tax Advisors as well as Internal and External Auditors. You will also work with internal compliance and drive improvement projects. This requires a sharp eye and genuine interest for accounting both from a legal and managerial standpoint!

As JBT AB is owned by JBT Marel, a US listed company, it requires a strict internal control environment. JBT has complied with the SOx program (Sarbanes Oxley Act) since the beginning of 2002. This role will give you the opportunity to learn and to broaden your knowledge within internal control and processes as well as the accounting differences between the Swedish and US Gaap.

Qualifications

Our focus is to find the right person who can continue to grow together with us and develop the company. However, it is preferrable if you have a solid knowledge of the Swedish Financial reporting standard K3.

As a person, you are used to work independently with a clear structure and an attention to details. You meet deadlines and like to work within an environment that constantly change. We see that you have a high integrity as well as a flexible team player. The role includes several social touchpoints (both internally and externally) and therefore you must like to cooperate and have good social skills.

To be successful in this role, we believe that you have the following profile:

  • An academic degree within relevant field, for example in Business Administration - Accounting or similar education
  • 3 to 5 years’ experience of qualified accounting or similar
  • Strong knowledge in the Swedish Financial reporting standard K3
  • Very good knowledge in Excel
  • Fluent in both Swedish and English, written and verbally
  • Knowledge of US Gaap as well as SOx program would be the cherry on top and seen as a strong merit, but it is not a requirement - so don’t let this stop you from applying!


We are looking forward to reviewing your application!

In JBT, we welcome a diverse and inclusive environment. This is key when it comes to innovation and finding new solutions.

Why work at JBT ...

We offer you the opportunity to work in an established international and fast-growing company. It is important for us to create opportunities for our colleagues for personal development and a work environment where individual initiatives are valued. We have strong focus on continuous development and improvement, and always together as a team. The role is located in our office in Helsingborg, with the possibility to work from home two days a week.

companyDescription:
Cyber threats are becoming more and more difficult to identify. Today, many companies and organizations have a long way to go to protect themselves against corporate espionage and data manipulation. At the same time, we are establishing more connected products, services and thus creating an increased customer demand in the Cybersecurity area.

Redigo's overall objective is to become an internationally trusted brand and company in Cybersecurity with services ranging from strategies and tactical initiatives to operational efforts. We provide services for brand protection and loss prevention to assist and strengthen your security posture. We reduce the risks of incidents to your organization no matter which sector you reside in. We put your organization on top of your risk position.

Additionally, we want to make it easier for you to meet the increasing compliance requirements from regulatory authorities and international bodies.

So that you can focus on what matters most to you and your business.
companySlogan: We incorporate an engineering approach into Cybersecurity to better protect your products and services.
descriptionHtml:
Our Business Area Resilience & Readiness is expanding, and we're seeking new talent to join us on our exciting journey! 🚀

Working at Redigo means working at a young, international, and fast-paced Cybersecurity company that offers an ideal workplace for those who want to have a real impact on the growth of an organization and are craving for responsibilities. It also means having lots of fun!

Here at Redigo we apply a secure-by-design approach to Cybersecurity all while having a good time and sticking to our core values of being Aware, Agile and of course Amusing.

As an Information Security Advisor you'll enjoy:

  • The opportunity to work in the forefront of technology and development together with our clients in Defense, Aeronautics, Finance and Automotive industries, among others.
  • A competitive salary and benefits package including an annual bonus structure.
  • Growth opportunities in a fast-growing industry, including continuous educations and training for industry certifications through our Performance Development Plans.
  • Paid holidays and flexible hybrid or work from home schedules
  • Team conferences, annual team trip, Holiday / Christmas Party and weekly quizzes
  • A Cyber tech kit that includes a MacBook or PC, iPhone and plan. Hey, maybe even an awesome hoodie and matching socks.

We value your personality and passion!

At Redigo, we prioritize personality and passion over formal education and lots of industry certifications (though they are nice to have as well). We believe in giving you ownership of your work, so working remotely is not an issue for you. Your personality, passion, hunger for learning and growth, and ability to work well with others are what matter most to us. As a member of our team, you'll have the opportunity to develop your skills with the support of your colleagues. With the right attitude and mindset, you'll thrive and achieve success together with your team. 🌟

Additionally, we're seeking someone who:

  • Has 3+ years or more experience working within Information Security industry.
  • Has experience in end to end internal and external audit certification programs to include ISO 27001 and NIST 800-53.
  • Can demonstrate knowledge of regulatory standards and requirements such as PCI-DSS, Cyber Resilience Act, NIS2, SOC 2, ISO/SAE 21434, R155/R156, DORA, GDPR, KSF/ISM, FAR/DFARS and CMMC.
  • Experience in a large enterprise Information Security Programs or Product Security and Governance programs.
  • Experience in implementing processes and methods for auditing and addressing non-compliance to information security standards; facilitates migration of non-compliant environments to compliant environments.
  • Experience in conducting risk assessments with organizations to ensure compliance with standards and currency with industry security norms.
  • Knows how to manage cyber risk and can communicate technical geek talk to business leaders or stakeholders.
  • Has worked in teams or with managing team(s) and project(s) for Information Security management.
  • Ability to develop and maintain effective relationships with stakeholders, customers and project team members.
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills and presentation skills
  • Is proactive and self-motivated, maintaining high levels of activity
  • Possesses a positive and caring attitude

Please note that work tests and background checks of applicants may be conducted during the recruitment process. This will include checking your references and previous employment. Though we do trust what you say we must verify as we are a Cybersecurity Company.

If this describes you, then you know what to do!

Good luck!

companyDescription:
ABOUT US

IsaDora is Sweden’s no.1 make up brand and since 1983 we have brought honest makeup to the many. We want to empower everyone to set their own beauty standards by offering affordable, high-quality, and easy-to-use products, without compromise. Guided by Swedish timeless values, our no-nonsense approach means that our premium products that do what they say, are safe in every way and accessible to everyone, no matter the amount of time at hand or size of wallet. Our Swedish roots are the moral compass that helps us stay inclusive, transparent, and responsible in everything we do. With IsaDora you don’t have to compromise on being you. That’s what honest beauty means to us.
descriptionHtml:
Join Our Team as Senior Accountant!

Are you a detail-driven, analytical finance professional with a passion for structure and precision? Then maybe you are our new colleague!

Isadora is a Swedish founded makeup brand. We are based in Malmö were we also have our production facilities. The brand has existed for more than 40 years and remains the no 1 makeup brand in Sweden and is also present in both Nordic, European and International markets.

We are now looking for someone who thrives in operational work and takes pride in delivering high-quality financial reporting.

Key Responsibilites:


  • Manage day-to-day accounting operations
  • Lead monthly, quarterly, and annual closing processes, including VAT-reporting and tax declarations
  • Ownership of the external audit process, including coordination and communication with external auditors
  • Ensure compliance with internal and external financial regulations
  • Drive efficiencies and improvement projects within the Finance organization
  • Collaborate across departments to support financial transparency and accuracy


The role is based in our head office in Malmö.

Qualifications/Profile:


  • Master's degree in Business Administration or equivalent
  • Broad experience in accounting and financial reporting
  • Strong knowledge of closing procedures, audit processes and tax declarations
  • A proactive, independent mindset with a hands-on approach
  • Excellent analytical skills and attention to detail
  • Strong ability to manage Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central or solid knowledge of similar systems
  • Ability to learn new programs and drive improvement through technology
  • Fluent in English


What We Offer:


  • A chance to join Sweden’s no.1 makeup brand!
  • A flexible working place where your opinion matters, and you can make real impact
  • The opportunity to be part of a growth journey within an established company with strong values
  • You'll be a part of a dedicated Finance team that values accuracy, teamwork, and continuous improvement while we are having fun along the way!


At IsaDora, we believe it is all about mindset, great skills and attitude. If you are aligned to our core values; to be honest and empowering, without compromise, are excited about the opportunity and great at what you do; driven by high targets and thrive in a fast-paced environment, we would love to hear from you. Apply now to join our team!

We review applications and send invitations for interviews on an ongoing basis, so do not wait to apply.

descriptionHtml:

  • Apply To Position
  • Use My Indeed Resume

Plant Controller/Finance Manager - Skara Plant

Are you ready for a new challenge as a controller in a new company where you have the chance to influence and grow? We are looking for someone who is curious, independent and has good skills in practical economics and likes challenges. We have an opportunity for you now as we enter a new company structure and create new conditions for an exciting future.

Main Purpose of the Role

  • Business Partner to the Plant General Manager with focus on driving the plant to achieving Budget EBITDA & Cash Flow
  • Active member of the plant leadership team, involved in all aspects of the plants operational and strategic decision making
  • Full finance responsibility for the plants Income Statement and Balance Sheet
  • Finance leader for the plant, with direct management with responsibility training and development of the finance team
  • Commercial finance focus to establish the competitive profit levels for all customers and products for current and future business integrated into the plants LRP (5 years business outlook)
  • Plant Finance leader reporting directly into regional Finance Manager

Main Missions & Results

  • Ensure unfailing support to all Health & Safety initiatives and drive the appropriate behaviors to build and maintain a safe and ergonomic workplace
  • Plant Finance leader reporting directly into regional Finance Manager
  • Lead and develop the Finance and Controlling department’s activities so that the plant delivers Financial reports on schedule and within quality standards.
  • Co-ordinating shared service inputs into the plant financial statements with responsibility for impact on plants Income Statement and Balance Sheet
  • Deliver accurate and complete financial data throughout the monthly finance cycle (forecast - flash - close) in-line with corporate timetable
  • Monitor key performance measures against agreed targets and detail as part of weekly and monthly reviews, communicate financial performance to GM and plant team to drive and support achievement of financial objectives
  • Communicate financial performance to regional and European leadership team with clear explanations of business performance and financial impacts
  • Complete variance analysis in Income Statement walk allocating variances to company 'bridge' categories
  • Calculate and report accurate and complete financial data for customer and product profitability analysis with focus on DVP (direct variable profit)
  • Review and approve price changes and calculate impact on material margin, ensure profit levels do not deteriorate
  • Ensure DSS (Direct source supply) sales and material pricing is aligned to customer agreement. Ensure this alignment is maintained during changes, including impact on retrospective price changes
  • Review and challenge all labour and overhead costs with plant team to drive down costs
  • "Walk the shop floor" to actively identify continuous improvement costs reductions and value additions
  • Prepare capital investment appraisal and participate in regional capex reviews
  • Calculate and report accurate and complete financial data to support projects as required
  • Full responsibility for plants budget and LRP generation and updates, co-ordinate plant inputs, lead financial reviews and support GM with financial analysis and presentations
  • Ensure correct classification and timing of revenues and costs into Income Statement
  • Ensure accurate reporting of assets and liabilities on balance sheet.
  • Review and approve the plant Balance sheet reconciliations monthly, ensuring they are completed on time, accurately and with full financial integrity
  • Responsible for supporting internal and external audits and ensuring that data is provided inline with audit timelines
  • Submission of 13 week cashflow each week with clear understanding of underlying inputs
  • Reporting of key working capital metrics (DSO, DMO and DOH)
  • Monitor AR balance accuracy with focus on aged receivables to ensure collection of debt
  • Monitor AP and GRNI balances accuracy with focus on ageing profile
  • Monitor inventory balances accuracy with focus on result of cycle counting
  • Calculation of standard costs annually
  • Support commercial sales team on new business quotations and current program changes to drive profitability to levels at or above company targets.
  • Regular detailed review of costs and profitability of key launches against budget assumptions and program management deliverables
  • Complete monthly financial package and present in Plant Management reviews
  • Leadership of finance team and support with specific projects and problem-solving activities.

Qualifications

Preferred Experience and Education

  • Qualified Accountant with experience in high volume (preferably automotive) manufacturing environment.
  • Educated to high level, Degree qualified - desirable

Experience

  • Previous financial management experience within similar industry & scale of operations.
  • 5 years+ experience in a finance leadership position in a manufacturing environment
  • Successful track record in leading and implementing projects, techniques and end to end methodologies.
  • Successful track record in implementing and developing new processes and changes in mid seize plants.
  • Full understanding & proven ability to meet near & long-term goals.
  • Previous experience of and industry with complex material flow and large labor force.
  • Ability to effectively operate, influence and communicate with employees.
  • Fluent written and spoken English/Swedish.

Managerial / Personal Skills

  • Proven interpersonal and influencing skills especially in a cross functional team.
  • High level of drive and personal commitment.
  • Working well under pressure and engaging at all levels of the organization both internally and externally.
  • Well-developed leadership skills with excellent influencing capabilities.
  • Ability to make and accept challenge positively and debate confidently.
  • Credible with peers and able to demonstrate added value.
  • High problem-solving skills with drive for continuous improvement.
  • High degree of integrity always prepared to do the right thing.

companyDescription:
Alvarez & Marsal is a leading global professional services firm dedicated to helping organizations tackle their most complex business issues, maximize stakeholder value, and deliver sustainable change. 

Privately held since its founding in 1983, clients select us for our deep expertise and proven ability to create and deliver practical solutions to their unique problems. Leveraging A&M’s restructuring heritage, our fact-driven, action-oriented approach empowers organizations to drive transformation and unlock value at every stage of growth.

Our worldwide network extends across six continents, with over 10,000 people comprised of experienced operators, world-class consultants, former regulators and industry authorities. We foster a uniquely collaborative environment that embraces our peoples’ diverse perspectives and A&M’s entrepreneurial spirit to deliver end-to-end capabilities that span advisory, business performance improvement, and turnaround management.

At A&M, our people are our greatest strength. Their talent, drive, diverse experiences and backgrounds enable us to solve complex challenges, create value and deliver exceptional results for clients around the world. We take pride in maintaining a culture that rewards merit, encourages initiative and supports continuous professional growth. We are committed to cultivating an inclusive work environment that supports creativity, collaboration and meaningful engagement, factors we believe are essential to delivering outstanding client outcomes and building fulfilling, long-term careers.

When action matters, find us at www.alvarezandmarsal.com.
descriptionHtml:
Assistant Director / Director (CMAA) – Global Transaction Advisory - Stockholm


About Alvarez & Marsal


Alvarez & Marsal (A&M) is a global consulting firm with over 10,000 entrepreneurial, action and results-oriented professionals in over 40 countries. We take a hands-on approach to solving our clients' problems and assisting them in reaching their potential. Our culture celebrates independent thinkers and doers who positively impact our clients and shape our industry. The collaborative environment and engaging work—guided by A&M's core values of Integrity, Quality, Objectivity, Fun, Personal Reward, and Inclusive Diversity—are why our people love working at A&M.


The Team


Join Alvarez & Marsal’s dynamic Capital Markets and Accounting Advisory (CMAA) team, a part of our award-winning Transaction Advisory practice with over 1,200 professionals in more than 40 offices worldwide. Our Transaction Advisory team is recognized as a leading provider of transaction services globally, working on a wide range of deals across various sectors. We offer a fulfilling career path in CMAA/Transaction Advisory, with opportunities to work on high-profile projects for Private Equity and corporate clients, hedge funds, sovereign wealth funds and Family offices around the world and develop professionally within a supportive and inclusive environment.


Our Capital Markets and Accounting Advisory team assists companies in every step of a capital market journey. We work closely together with our clients and help them identify, design and implement value added solutions whether it is in the phase of IPO preparations, IPO execution, spin-offs, transactions or ensuring that capital markets expectations and requirements are met on a regular basis when operating as a listed company.


We advise our customers in a wide range of areas such as corporate governance, risk management, internal control, financial management, operational excellence, internal and external information, information security and IT, transaction accounting and financial reporting as well as sustainability reporting and governance.


CMAA is growing and as we are continuing to expand our established Nordic team in Stockholm we are seeking an Assistant Director/Director who is looking to take the next transformative career step. In this pivotal role, you will take active part in business development, driving growth and innovation while delivering exceptional client services. We are seeking a relationship-driven team player who values and thrives in a collaborative environment, is flexible in adapting to evolving challenges and is passionate about our entrepreneurial mindset.


How you will contribute


As an Assistant Director/Director within our CMAA team, you will provide advice and support in the following areas:


  • Development of future-proof, highly digital and efficient finance organizations
  • Financial planning and follow-up processes (target setting, KPIs, business planning, budgeting and internal monthly reporting follow-up)
  • Governance and internal control structures (as required of a listed company)
  • ESG and sustainability readiness – CSRD, governance and organizational structuring, processes and reporting
  • Project management (incl. questions pertaining to processes, requirements and filings in connection with IPOs, capital raisings, carve-outs and other national and international capital markets transactions).


You will also:


  • Work with a variety of large-cap and mid-cap PE houses, corporate clients and financial institutions providing broad industry & client exposure
  • Contribute to and lead capital markets projects
  • Contribute to and lead business development initiatives
  • Contribute and lead external marketing and sales initiatives


Qualifications


  • University degree, preferably in Business, Finance, Economics or Law – Bachelors or Masters degree
  • At least 5 years working experience from capital markets transaction advisory services, management consulting or risk/internal control advisory
  • Experience within audit or advisory services at a leading consultancy firm
  • Experience from working within a finance organization (Finance Operations, FP&A, Accounting or Treasury) is considered to be highly meriting
  • Strong project management skills
  • Strong interpersonal, written, and oral communication skills with an ability to assimilate easily into teams
  • Proactive in identifying client needs and effective in building a strong relationship with clients
  • Excellent consulting skills: analytical, written and verbal communication, client presence, facilitation and presentation skills
  • High energy with commitment to quality client service, focusing on ensuring positive resolutions to conflicts
  • Word, Excel and PowerPoint
  • High proficiency in English and Swedish (both written and orally) is essential. Other European languages are highly desirable.
  • Last but not least, we are looking for a team-player who has an entrepreneurial nature, strong drive and who is ethical and dependable.


Your journey at A&M


We recognize that our people are the driving force behind our success, which is why we prioritize an employee experience that fosters each person’s unique professional and personal development. Our robust performance development process promotes continuous learning, rewards your contributions, and fosters a culture of meritocracy. With top-notch training and on-the-job learning opportunities, you can acquire new skills and advance your career.


We prioritize your well-being, providing benefits and resources to support you on your personal journey. Our people consistently highlight the growth opportunities, our unique, entrepreneurial culture, and the fun we have together as their favorite aspects of working at A&M. The possibilities are endless for high-performing and passionate professionals


Inclusive Diversity

A&M’s entrepreneurial culture celebrates independent thinkers and doers who can positively impact our clients and shape our industry. The collaborative environment and engaging work—guided by A&M’s core values of Integrity, Quality, Objectivity, Fun, Personal Reward, and Inclusive Diversity—are the main reasons our people love working at A&M. Inclusive Diversity means we embrace diversity, and we foster inclusiveness, encouraging everyone to bring their whole self to work each day. It runs through how we recruit, develop employees, conduct business, support clients, and partner with vendors. It is the A&M way.


Equal Opportunity Employer

It is Alvarez & Marsal’s practice to provide and promote equal opportunity in employment, compensation, and other terms and conditions of employment without discrimination because of race, color, creed, religion, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, sex or gender, gender identity or gender expression (including transgender status), sexual orientation, marital status, military service and veteran status, physical or mental disability, family medical history, genetic information or other protected medical condition, political affiliation, or any other characteristic protected by and in accordance with applicable laws. Employees and Applicants can find A&M policy statements and additional information by region here.


Unsolicited Resumes from Third-Party Recruiters

Please note that as per A&M policy, we do not accept unsolicited resumes from third-party recruiters unless such recruiters are engaged to provide candidates for a specified opening. Any employment agency, person or entity that submits an unsolicited resume does so with the understanding that A&M will have the right to hire that applicant at its discretion without any fee owed to the submitting employment agency, person or entity.

companyDescription:
Air Liquide is a world leader in gases, technologies and services for industry and healthcare. Present in 72 countries with 67,800 employees, the Group serves more than 4 million customers and patients. Oxygen, nitrogen and hydrogen are essential small molecules for life, matter and energy. They embody Air Liquide’s scientific territory and have been at the core of the Group’s activities since its creation in 1902.

Taking action today while preparing the future is at the heart of Air Liquide’s strategy. With ADVANCE, its strategic plan for 2025, Air Liquide is targeting a global performance, combining financial and extra-financial dimensions. Positioned on new markets, the Group benefits from major assets such as its business model combining resilience and strength, its ability to innovate and its technological expertise. The Group develops solutions contributing to climate and the energy transition—particularly with hydrogen—and takes action to progress in areas of healthcare, digital and high technologies.

Air Liquide’s revenue amounted to more than 27.5 billion euros in 2023. Air Liquide is listed on the Euronext Paris stock exchange (compartment A) and belongs to the CAC 40, CAC 40 ESG, EURO STOXX 50, FTSE4Good and DJSI Europe indexes.
descriptionHtml:
How will you CONTRIBUTE and GROW?

Are you a seasoned finance professional looking to lead a dynamic team and make a real impact? We're seeking a Finance BP Manager to oversee our Nordic finance operations.

In this role, you will:

Lead and mentor a team of 4 Finance Business Partners and a Nordic Credit Manager.

Ensure full statutory and tax compliance for our 5 Nordic legal entities.

Drive efficient collaboration with our Business Service Center (BSC).

Coordinate and manage all audit activities.

Act as the primary contact for insurance and legal matters across the Nordic region.

  • Financial closing; the group & statutory reporting in time
  • Tax compliance in Nordics (VAT, Income tax, Transfer pricing)
  • Support legal entities in any accounting tax questions
  • Support internal control compliance
  • Lead local audits and ensure the companies governance
  • Leading interaction towards BSC (Business Service Center):
    • Develop meeting structure / issue resolution approach with ALEBS (Air Liquide Europe Business Services)
    • Ensure ALEBS performance and KPIs are in place and regularly followed up and actions taken as needed. Set the common areas to improve with ALEBS
  • Team performance
    • Review task and time allocation of the team
    • Assess and design split of activities by person
    • Develop working methods and tools


___________________

Are you a MATCH?

  • Master's degree in Economics, Accounting, or a related field.
  • 5+ years of accounting expertise, ideally gained in audit firms or similar dynamic environments. You're a pro at accounting methods!
  • Demonstrated ability to lead and inspire teams in an international setting
  • Basic knowledge of SAP (Hana preferred and proficiency in consolidation tools
  • Team player who excels at managing demanding deadlines
  • Fluent in Swedish, other Nordic languages is a plus. This role is based in Malmö


Our Differences make our Performance

At Air Liquide, we are committed to build a diverse and inclusive workplace that embraces the diversity of our employees, our customers, patients, community stakeholders and cultures across the world.

We welcome and consider applications from all qualified applicants, regardless of their background. We strongly believe a diverse organization opens up opportunities for people to express their talent, both individually and collectively and it helps foster our ability to innovate by living our fundamentals, acting for our success and creating an engaging environment in a changing world.

companyDescription:
Overal van betekenis, dát is aaff. Geboren uit ABAB en Alfa, kom je ons met ruim 50 vestigingen overal tegen. We zijn graag dichtbij én werken het liefst mét mensen en vóór mensen. We zijn ambitieus en willen jou helpen om het beste uit je organisatie te halen. Dat doen we onder meer op het gebied van accountancy, audit & assurance, fiscaal advies, bedrijfsadvies én juridisch advies. aaff is lokaal betrokken en wij delen onze kennis graag, daar staan én gaan we voor. 

Als werkgever maken we het graag persoonlijk. Daarom hebben we geen partnerstructuur. Bij aaff zijn we met alle 2000 collega’s voor 100% eigenaar. We zorgen voor een inclusieve werkomgeving en zetten in op werkplezier en een goede work-life balance. Daarnaast investeren we volop in opleiding, ontwikkeling, kennis en innovatie. Tegelijkertijd willen we op allerlei manieren bijdragen aan een betere wereld. Echt impact maken! Het zit diepgeworteld in onze genen. 

Kom je werken bij aaff en ga je ook voor échte betekenis? Ontdek alle mogelijkheden op www.aaff.nl.

descriptionHtml:
Wat krijg je van ons?

  • Salaris: tot €4309.
  • Co-eigenaarschap vanaf dag één (bij vast contract): je krijgt tien certificaten cadeau en kunt meerdere keren per jaar extra certificaten bijkopen.
  • Regie over je werktijden: werk thuis of op kantoor, in overleg met je team.
  • Keuzevrijheid in verlof: tot 38 vrije dagen per jaar.

Wat vragen we van je?

  • Kennis en ervaring: je hebt net een hbo-opleiding afgerond (of bent hier mee bezig) in Accountancy, Finance & Control of een vergelijkbare richting.
  • Skills en vaardigheden: je kunt zelfstandig werken en taken snel oppakken, terwijl jouw eerlijkheid en openheid je communicatief sterk maken.
  • Mentaliteit: je hebt een klantgerichte en proactieve houding, je denkt graag mee over de bedrijfsvoering van onze klanten én hebt de mentaliteit van een co-eigenaar.

Assistent accountant mkb

Locatie aaff Ede

Tijd 24 - 40 uren per week

Level Young professional

Solliciteer direct!

Assistent accountant in Ede

Aangenaam, wij zijn aaff. De nieuwe naam in accountancy en advies met maar liefst 180 jaar ervaring. Bij ons ben je meer dan een werknemer; je bent één van de 2000 co-eigenaren. Met onze participatieregeling kun jij elk jaar investeren en zo meedelen in onze winst. Winst die er zonder jou niet was geweest. Maar co-eigenaarschap is meer dan certificaten alleen. Het is een houding, een gevoel. Ze zeggen ook wel: ‘run it, like you own it’. En dat is wat wij doen. Wij zoeken een assistent accountant die binnen aaff het verschil gaat maken voor onze klanten. Iemand die zich wil ontwikkelen in een dynamisch team en wil bijdragen aan zowel zijn eigen groei als die van aaff. Word jij éen van onze 2000 co-eigenaren?

Wat ga je doen als assistent accountant?

Als assistent accountant bij aaff werk je in een dynamische omgeving waar je veel kunt leren en groeien. Je ondersteunt je collega's bij het op orde houden van administraties en het voorbereiden van jaarrekeningen. Daarnaast help je ondernemers met fiscale aangiftes, tussentijdse cijfers, het opstellen van jaarrekeningen en optimaliseer je de administratie. Je krijgt veel verantwoordelijkheid en vrijheid in je werk en je werkt zelfstandig met klanten. Vanaf dag één krijg je een vaste begeleider die je meeneemt en inwerkt. Je komt in aanraking met diverse sectoren, zoals transport, horeca, hightech/maakindustrie en de bouw, wat het werk gevarieerd en uitdagend maakt.

De sfeer bij aaff is open, informeel en betrokken. Bij aaff bouwen we samen aan een betekenisvolle toekomst en duurzame relaties. Of je nu werkt met start-ups, familiebedrijven of gevestigde ondernemingen. Bij aaff krijg je meer dan een baan: je bouwt mee als co-eigenaar. 

Je Belangrijkste Verantwoordelijkheden Zijn

  • Controleren en verwerken van administraties dmv onze automatisering of onze scan en herkensoftware.
  • Opstellen van fiscale aangiften.
  • Optimaliseren van financiële administraties en automatisering.
  • Opstellen van jaarrekeningen en tussentijdse cijfers en overige administratieve stukken.
  • Jezelf ontwikkelen in het vak en bij aaff de mogelijkheid krijgen om door te groeien naar een meer ervaren functie óf door te groeien naar de functie van Accountant, als je die wens hebt, via het Young Professional Program. Er is altijd een passend opleidingsprogramma binnen aaff.

Jouw Arbeidsvoorwaarden

  • Salaris: tot € 4309 op fulltime basis, afhankelijk van jouw ervaring.
  • Keuzevrijheid in verlof: jij bepaalt hoeveel vrije dagen je opneemt, tot 38 per jaar. Kies uit regulier verlof, het aaff-budget of extra dagen via je salaris. Wat je van het aaff-budget niet gebruikt, keren we uit.
  • Ruimte om te leren tijdens werktijd: via onze aaff academie of een externe opleiding.
  • Duidelijk groeipad: met volop mogelijkheden om je te specialiseren of door te stromen.
  • Duurzaam onderweg: 0,30 per auto-km voor woon-werkritten, € 0,40 (€ 0,23 netto) per fiets-/wandelkilometer. Daarbovenop een volledige OV-vergoeding of – bij meer dan 7.500 zakelijke km/jaar – een elektrische leaseauto of mobiliteitsbudget. Bij elektrisch rijden ontvang je € 0,40 per kilometer.
  • Vergoeding voor thuiswerken: € 2,40 per thuiswerkdag én € 40 netto internetvergoeding per maand.
  • Thuiswerkbudget van € 550: om je werkplek thuis prettig en ergonomisch in te richten.
  • Jaarlijks € 200 vitaliteitsbudget: voor jouw mentale en fysieke gezondheid én altijd toegang tot het online platform OpenUp.
  • Flexibel verlof sparen: voor een sabbatical, langere reis of tijd voor jezelf.
  • Feestdagen omruilen: kies twee dagen die passen bij jouw religie of cultuur.
  • Laptop en telefoon: alles wat je nodig hebt om jouw werk goed te doen.

Is deze vacature iets voor jou?

We zijn benieuwd naar jouw verhaal en horen graag hoe jij ons als co-eigenaar komt versterken. Heb je nog vragen? Schroom niet om contact op te nemen met Alissa Vogelsang, onze regio recruiter, via alissa.vogelsang@aaff.nl of 06 39 23 48 71. Of solliciteer direct.

Solliciteer nu

Is deze vacature iets voor jou?

Word onderdeel van ons team en solliciteer nu! We leren je graag kennen.

Sollicitatieformulier (externe link, opent in nieuw venster)

Solliciteer direct!

Aangenaam, aaff.

Hét gezicht in accountancy en advies met 2.000 co-eigenaren. Samen ondersteunen we bedrijven in financiën, fiscaliteit, juridische advisering en bedrijfsvoering. Als co-eigenaar bouw je vanaf dag één mee aan ons gezamenlijke succes. Je denkt mee, ziet kansen en voelt je verbonden met aaff omdat je er zelf onderdeel van bent. Die mentaliteit merk je overal: geen hiërarchie of dichte deuren, maar korte lijnen, vertrouwen en samenwerking. Met 50 vestigingen door heel Nederland zijn we altijd dichtbij. Betrokkenheid om de hoek, maar met impact die verder reikt. Zo bouwen we lokaal aan iets groots – voor ondernemers, voor elkaar en voor de toekomst.

Aangenaam

Aangenaam

Sezgin - relatiemanager / bedrijfsadviseur - Ede / Arnhem

We spreken met één van de 2.000 co-eigenaren van aaff: Sezgin. Hij werkt bijna twee jaar bij ons op kantoor in Ede en Arnhem. Na 11 jaar ondernemerschap heeft hij gekozen voor een vast dienstverband als relatiemanager / bedrijfsadviseur bij aaff, en dit bevalt hem uitstekend. Sezgin beheert klantrelaties, geeft ze strategische adviezen en werft nieuwe klanten. In dit interview vertelt hij over zijn overstap en de flexibiliteit die hij ervaart om werk te combineren met zijn vele hobby's.

Lees verder

Twijfels over de functie, maar wel interesse in werken bij aaff?

Vraag dan een vrijblijvend kennismakingsgesprek aan! Of laat een open sollicitatie achter.

Vrijblijvend kennismaken Open sollicitatie

Bij aaff krijg je meer dan een baan: je bouwt mee als co-eigenaar. Je Belangrijkste Verantwoordelijkheden ZijnControleren en verwerken van administraties dmv onze automatisering of onze scan en herkensoftware.Opstellen van fiscale aangiften.Optimaliseren van financiële administraties en automatisering.Opstellen van jaarrekeningen en tussentijdse cijfers en overige administratieve stukken.Jezelf ontwikkelen in het vak en bij aaff de mogelijkheid krijgen om door te groeien naar een meer ervaren functie óf door te groeien naar de functie van Accountant, als je die wens hebt, via het Young Professional Program. Er is altijd een passend opleidingsprogramma binnen aaff.Jouw ArbeidsvoorwaardenSalaris: tot € 4309 op fulltime basis, afhankelijk van jouw ervaring. Keuzevrijheid in verlof: jij bepaalt hoeveel vrije dagen je opneemt, tot 38 per jaar. Kies uit regulier verlof, het aaff-budget of extra dagen via je salaris. Wat je van het aaff-budget niet gebruikt, keren we uit. Ruimte om te leren tijdens werktijd: via onze aaff academie of een externe opleiding. Duidelijk groeipad: met volop mogelijkheden om je te specialiseren of door te stromen. Duurzaam onderweg: 0,30 per auto-km voor woon-werkritten, € 0,40 (€ 0,23 netto) per fiets-/wandelkilometer. Daarbovenop een volledige OV-vergoeding of – bij meer dan 7.500 zakelijke km/jaar – een elektrische leaseauto of mobiliteitsbudget. Bij elektrisch rijden ontvang je € 0,40 per kilometer. Vergoeding voor thuiswerken: € 2,40 per thuiswerkdag én € 40 netto internetvergoeding per maand. Thuiswerkbudget van € 550: om je werkplek thuis prettig en ergonomisch in te richten. Jaarlijks € 200 vitaliteitsbudget: voor jouw mentale en fysieke gezondheid én altijd toegang tot het online platform OpenUp. Flexibel verlof sparen: voor een sabbatical, langere reis of tijd voor jezelf. Feestdagen omruilen: kies twee dagen die passen bij jouw religie of cultuur. Laptop en telefoon: alles wat je nodig hebt om jouw werk goed te doen. Is deze vacature iets voor jou? We zijn benieuwd naar jouw verhaal en horen graag hoe jij ons als co-eigenaar komt versterken. Heb je nog vragen? Schroom niet om contact op te nemen met Alissa Vogelsang, onze regio recruiter, via alissa.vogelsang@aaff.nl of 06 39 23 48 71. Of solliciteer direct.Solliciteer nuIs deze vacature iets voor jou?Word onderdeel van ons team en solliciteer nu! We leren je graag kennen. Samen ondersteunen we bedrijven in financiën, fiscaliteit, juridische advisering en bedrijfsvoering. Als co-eigenaar bouw je vanaf dag één mee aan ons gezamenlijke succes. Je denkt mee, ziet kansen en voelt je verbonden met aaff omdat je er zelf onderdeel van bent. Die mentaliteit merk je overal: geen hiërarchie of dichte deuren, maar korte lijnen, vertrouwen en samenwerking. Met 50 vestigingen door heel Nederland zijn we altijd dichtbij. Betrokkenheid om de hoek, maar met impact die verder reikt. Zo bouwen we lokaal aan iets groots – voor ondernemers, voor elkaar en voor de toekomst.AangenaamAangenaamSezgin - relatiemanager / bedrijfsadviseur - Ede / ArnhemWe spreken met één van de 2.000 co-eigenaren van aaff: Sezgin. Hij werkt bijna twee jaar bij ons op kantoor in Ede en Arnhem. Sezgin beheert klantrelaties, geeft ze strategische adviezen en werft nieuwe klanten. In dit interview vertelt hij over zijn overstap en de flexibiliteit die hij ervaart om werk te combineren met zijn vele hobby's. companyDescription:
Wir bauen.
Mit Leidenschaft.
Die Eiffage Infra-Bau SE bringt mit ihren Tochterunternehmen mehr als 150 Jahre Erfahrung im Bauen mit – deutschlandweit sowie im europäischen Ausland und in Asien. Mit knapp 4.000 kompetenten, qualifizierten Mitarbeitern in Deutschland planen und bauen wir, betreiben Infrastrukturen und produzieren Baustoffe. Mit Sitz in Düsseldorf sind wir mit unseren Gesellschaften stark regional verankert und mit den drei Geschäftsbereichen Ingenieurbau, Sonderprojekte/PPP sowie Betrieb und Erhaltung vertreten. Von der Planung bis hin zur Umsetzung integrieren wir alle Gewerke aus einer Hand – und realisieren Projekte bis ins Detail. Unsere Arbeit ist sowohl von Wissen und Erfahrung geprägt als auch von der Leidenschaft für das Gelingen des großen Ganzen. 
Mit unseren Sechs Unternehmenswerten Vorbild, Vertrauen, Urteilsvermögen, Verantwortung, Offenheit und Engagement verkörpern wir genau das, was wir sein wollen: Eine Unternehmensgruppe, die basierend auf ihrem Anspruch an hohe Qualität und kollegiales Miteinander eine gemeinsame nachhaltige Zukunft gestaltet.
Im Jahr 2024 erwirtschaftete Eiffage Infra-Bau mit seinen 4.000 Mitarbeitenden in 14 Leistungsbereichen eine Gesamtleistung von 1,25 Milliarden Euro und ist Teil der Infrastruktursparte Eiffage Infrastructures des französischen Eiffage-Konzerns (Umsatz: 23,4 Milliarden Euro, 79.400 Mitarbeiter). 
Haben Sie Interesse? Dann steigen Sie gerne bei uns ein unter www.eiffage-infra.de/karriere

descriptionHtml:
Gesellschaft: Eiffage Infra-Nordic

Standort

Göteborg, SE, 412 50

Arbeitsbereich: Finanzen/Controlling/Rechnungswesen

Vertragsart: Unbefristet

Controller (m/f/d)

Eiffage Infra-Nordic AB, headquartered in Helsingborg, Sweden, with further branches in Göteborg and Stockholm, is a specialist in infrastructure construction. The company offers a broad portfolio of services in the areas of track and rail construction and civil engineering. Eiffage Infra-Nordic is part of Eiffage Infra-Bau in Germany. Eiffage Infra-Nordic is currently in the developing phase and offers new employees interesting career options in an atmosphere characterized by growth.

We are offering the following vacancy for Göteborg with a start as soon as possible: Controller (m/f/d)

With the scale of growth, our Commercial Manager has more to coordinate, correspond, prioritize, prepare, answer… - and that is why we need you, who can professionally and smoothly help her and take over topics in the field of controlling.

In this position you have the whole area to manage, and your mission is to develop and secure our work with quality and structure. Are you experienced in controlling and have a background in accounting and can take on this task? We wish to meet you.

What You Will Do

  • Responsible for controlling topics for Eiffage Infra-Nordic AB, reporting to Commercial Manager
  • Project controlling and support to our Project Managers
  • Prepare reports for monthly progress and quarterly forecasts
  • Check for, analyze and explain deviations
  • Prepare and oversee that monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements are in line with local GAAP and group policies
  • Review and controlling of all financial figures
  • Work with local GAAP, IFRS and fast close
  • Prepare and assist in annual audit, K3 annual report
  • Responsible for country relevant statistical returns and reports
  • Support and assist with other tasks within the area of Commercial/Financial

What are we looking for?

  • Successfully completed studies at university in Business Administration, Economics, Finance or similar
  • Relevant experience in controlling, ideally experience in working with the controlling department of a foreign parent company
  • Background and experience in accounting
  • Good knowledge of relevant accounting systems, SAP FI CO, ideally S/4 Hana and Fiori
  • Very good skills in MS Office and especially excel with pivot, analyzes tool, and formulas
  • Disciplined and organized with the ability to manage and prioritize many tasks, always with a team player attitude
  • Strong analytical skills and a good sense of numbers
  • Positive attitude: approach work with enthusiasm and energy
  • Problem solving: ability to identify and solve problems quickly and effectively
  • Get stuff done attitude: you are not afraid of rolling up your sleeves and getting things done
  • Constant learner: actively seek new knowledge and skills
  • Convincing and strong communicative skills
  • Fluent in English and Swedish. English is our business language
  • Knowledge of German is of preference

What are we offering in addition to the salary?

  • Pension plan and insurance package according to union agreement
  • Welfare contribution

Why join us?

We're growing our business and are offering the opportunity to be part of our growth. Furthermore, you will be working with a diverse group talented, motivated, and prestige less co-workers. We emphasize getting stuff done, while having a lot of fun.

companyDescription:
Heidelberg Materials is one of the world's largest integrated manufacturers of building materials and solutions with leading market positions in cement, aggregates, and ready-mixed concrete. We are represented in around 50 countries with around 51,000 employees at almost 3,000 locations. At the centre of our actions lies the responsibility for the environment. As the front runner on the path to carbon neutrality and circular economy in the building materials industry, we are working on sustainable building materials and solutions for the future. We enable new opportunities for our customers through digitalisation.

descriptionHtml:
Vill du vara med och bidra till framtidens redovisning i ett bolag med historia, innovationstakt och samhällsviktiga uppdrag? Hos Heidelberg Materials får du både arbeta med kvalificerade redovisningsfrågor och vara del av ett team som vill utvecklas framåt. Vi söker nu en trygg och noggrann redovisningsansvarig som trivs i en analytisk roll och gillar att ha koll på både detaljer och helhet.


Rollen som Redovisningsansvarig

Du får ett helhetsgrepp kring redovisningen för bolaget – från det dagliga till årsbokslut och årsredovisning. Det innebär allt från att säkra månads-, kvartals- och årsbokslut enligt K3 och IFRS, till att fördjupa dig i koncernrapportering. Rollen är bred och kräver både självständighet, flexibilitet och ett analytiskt sinne.

  • Månads- och årsbokslut enligt K3 och IFRS
  • Rapportering till koncern (inkl. forecast och budget, balansräkning)
  • Moms, punktskatter och övriga deklarationer
  • Hantering av skatt och skatteberäkningar
  • Redovisning av anläggningstillgångar och investeringsprojekt
  • Leasingredovisning enligt IFRS 16
  • Upprättande av årsredovisningar enligt K3
  • Ansvara och driva förbättringar samt dokumentera och upprätta manualer
  • Intern kontroll och compliance


Vad söker vi?

Vi tror att du har minst fem års erfarenhet av kvalificerat redovisningsarbete, gärna i en större koncern med internationell närvaro. Du är bokslutssjälvständig, har erfarenhet av CAPEX, är trygg i att hantera skatte- och momsfrågor och har upprättat årsredovisning enligt K3. Du har även hanterat komplex koncernrapportering i större bolag. I grunden har du en ekonomiexamen eller motsvarande och du talar och skriver svenska och engelska obehindrat. Det är meriterande om du dessutom har erfarenhet av IFRS och erfarenhet av bolag med Shared Service Center.

Men erfarenhet är bara en del av den person vi söker. Du jobbar även strukturerat, ser detaljer, men har också förmågan att förstå och kommunicera helheten både till kollegor och externa parter. Du gillar att knäcka redovisningstekniska frågor, är lösningsorienterad, prestigelös och vågar ifrågasätta och föreslå förbättringar. Det är dessutom viktigt att du är självgående men samtidigt en lagspelare – här hjälps vi åt!

Varför Heidelberg Materials?

Hos Heidelberg Materials blir du en del av en global koncern med stark lokal närvaro och tydlig framtidsambition. Här kombineras stabilitet med förändringsvilja. Här finns goda möjligheter för utveckling, både för dig personligen och för bolaget. Vi sitter i ljusa och moderna lokaler vid Mälaren i Marievik, med närhet till både vatten, stad och kollegor. Arbetsmiljön präglas av öppenhet, engagemang och respekt – och vi jobbar aktivt med hållbarhet, innovation och utveckling.


Har vi väckt ditt intresse?

I denna rekrytering samarbetar Heidelbergs Materials med Mpya Finance. Urval och intervjuer sker löpande så skicka gärna in din ansökan snarast möjligt. Har du frågor gällande rekryteringsprocessen eller kring tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta ansvariga rekryteringskonsulter Elin Allskog, elin.allskog@mpya.se eller Maria Brisheim maria.brisheim@mpya.se. Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!


Heidelberg Materials är en del av Sveriges samhällsviktiga verksamhet. En del av våra tjänster är säkerhetsklassade. Är denna tjänst säkerhetsklassad kommer säkerhetsprövning genomföras innan anställning, i enlighet med Säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585)

descriptionHtml:
Plant Controller/Finance Manager - Låssby Plant

Are you ready for a new challenge as a controller in a new company where you have the chance to influence and grow? We are looking for someone who is curious, independent and has good skills in practical economics and likes challenges. We have an opportunity for you now as we enter a new company structure and create new conditions for an exciting future.

Main Purpose of the Role

  • Business Partner to the Plant General Manager with focus on driving the plant to achieving Budget EBITDA & Cash Flow
  • Active member of the plant leadership team, involved in all aspects of the plants operational and strategic decision making
  • Full finance responsibility for the plants Income Statement and Balance Sheet
  • Finance leader for the plant, with direct management with responsibility training and development of the finance team
  • Commercial finance focus to establish the competitive profit levels for all customers and products for current and future business integrated into the plants LRP (5 years business outlook)
  • Plant Finance leader reporting directly into regional Finance Manager

Main Missions & Results

  • Ensure unfailing support to all Health & Safety initiatives and drive the appropriate behaviors to build and maintain a safe and ergonomic workplace
  • Plant Finance leader reporting directly into regional Finance Manager
  • Lead and develop the Finance and Controlling department’s activities so that the plant delivers Financial reports on schedule and within quality standards.
  • Co-ordinating shared service inputs into the plant financial statements with responsibility for impact on plants Income Statement and Balance Sheet
  • Deliver accurate and complete financial data throughout the monthly finance cycle (forecast - flash - close) in-line with corporate timetable
  • Monitor key performance measures against agreed targets and detail as part of weekly and monthly reviews, communicate financial performance to GM and plant team to drive and support achievement of financial objectives
  • Communicate financial performance to regional and European leadership team with clear explanations of business performance and financial impacts
  • Complete variance analysis in Income Statement walk allocating variances to company 'bridge' categories
  • Calculate and report accurate and complete financial data for customer and product profitability analysis with focus on DVP (direct variable profit)
  • Review and approve price changes and calculate impact on material margin, ensure profit levels do not deteriorate
  • Ensure DSS (Direct source supply) sales and material pricing is aligned to customer agreement. Ensure this alignment is maintained during changes, including impact on retrospective price changes
  • Review and challenge all labour and overhead costs with plant team to drive down costs
  • "Walk the shop floor" to actively identify continuous improvement costs reductions and value additions
  • Prepare capital investment appraisal and participate in regional capex reviews
  • Calculate and report accurate and complete financial data to support projects as required
  • Full responsibility for plants budget and LRP generation and updates, co-ordinate plant inputs, lead financial reviews and support GM with financial analysis and presentations
  • Ensure correct classification and timing of revenues and costs into Income Statement
  • Ensure accurate reporting of assets and liabilities on balance sheet.
  • Review and approve the plant Balance sheet reconciliations monthly, ensuring they are completed on time, accurately and with full financial integrity
  • Responsible for supporting internal and external audits and ensuring that data is provided inline with audit timelines
  • Submission of 13 week cashflow each week with clear understanding of underlying inputs
  • Reporting of key working capital metrics (DSO, DMO and DOH)
  • Monitor AR balance accuracy with focus on aged receivables to ensure collection of debt
  • Monitor AP and GRNI balances accuracy with focus on ageing profile
  • Monitor inventory balances accuracy with focus on result of cycle counting
  • Calculation of standard costs annually
  • Support commercial sales team on new business quotations and current program changes to drive profitability to levels at or above company targets.
  • Regular detailed review of costs and profitability of key launches against budget assumptions and program management deliverables
  • Complete monthly financial package and present in Plant Management reviews
  • Leadership of finance team and support with specific projects and problem-solving activities.

Qualifications

Preferred Experience and Education

  • Qualified Accountant with experience in high volume (preferably automotive) manufacturing environment.
  • Educated to high level, Degree qualified - desirable

Experience

  • Previous financial management experience within similar industry & scale of operations.
  • 5 years+ experience in a finance leadership position in a manufacturing environment
  • Successful track record in leading and implementing projects, techniques and end to end methodologies.
  • Successful track record in implementing and developing new processes and changes in mid seize plants.
  • Full understanding & proven ability to meet near & long-term goals.
  • Previous experience of and industry with complex material flow and large labor force.
  • Ability to effectively operate, influence and communicate with employees.
  • Fluent written and spoken English/Swedish.

Managerial / Personal Skills

  • Proven interpersonal and influencing skills especially in a cross functional team.
  • High level of drive and personal commitment.
  • Working well under pressure and engaging at all levels of the organization both internally and externally.
  • Well-developed leadership skills with excellent influencing capabilities.
  • Ability to make and accept challenge positively and debate confidently.
  • Credible with peers and able to demonstrate added value.
  • High problem-solving skills with drive for continuous improvement.
  • High degree of integrity always prepared to do the right thing.

Jurek är ett rekryterings- och konsultföretaget som präglas av framåtanda.

Vi lägger mycket energi på att omsorgsfullt identifiera och matcha rätt kandidat till rätt uppdrag. Att gå på djupet "utanför CV:et"​ för att matcha rätt mellan kandidaten och det specifika uppdragets kultur och vision är vår absoluta ambition. Det värde vi lyckas leverera varje gång är också en av förklaringarna till att våra kunder återkommer gång på gång.
 
Vår affärsidé är att skapa en win-win situation för båda våra intressenter, d v s arbetstagare och uppdragsgivare. Detta uppnår vi genom att leverera flexibla och kundanpassade rekryterings- och bemanningslösningar över hela landet.
 
Vår vision är att bli det ledande företaget inom vår bransch och nisch, främst genom nöjda kunder och kandidater som hjälper oss att expandera!
